votes.reason: The Python code correctly implements the logic of the Coq specification, checking if the provided boolean result matches the condition that one integer is the sum of the other two.
The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification by checking if the result matches the boolean condition that one integer is the sum of the other two.
The Python code correctly evaluates the condition that one integer is the sum of the other two and checks if it matches the given result, exactly mirroring the Coq specification.

The Python code correctly computes the same boolean condition as the Coq spec: it checks whether any of the three integers equals the sum of the other two, and returns whether 'result' matches this condition. The Coq orb (||) of three Z.eq_dec expressions produces the same boolean as Python's 'or' of three equality checks.

The Python code correctly computes the same boolean condition as the Coq spec: it checks whether any of the three integers equals the sum of the other two, and returns whether 'result' matches this condition. The Coq orb (||) of three Z.eq_dec expressions produces the same boolean as Python's 'or' of three equality checks.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq spec. In Coq, the spec checks if result equals the disjunction of three equality checks (x == y+z, y == x+z, z == x+y). The Python code computes the same disjunction as 'condition' and returns whether 'result' equals this condition. Both produce the same boolean result for all concrete integer inputs.
Python computes whether one of x,y,z equals the sum of the other two and checks result matches this, using unbounded ints and boolean or, matching the Coq bool expression and equality over Z.
Both compute whether result equals ((x == y+z) or (y == x+z) or (z == x+y)); Python uses arbitrary-precision ints like Coq Z, so behavior matches on all inputs.
Both compute whether one integer equals the sum of the other two and return if the provided result matches that boolean; Python ints match Coq Z on concrete values.
